description: Component governance lifecycle for the AI-native design system. No component may be created without human approval.

phases.name: Proposal
phases.description: New component need is identified and formally proposed by any team member or AI agent

requiredFields.name: Component name in lowercase-hyphenated format
requiredFields.displayName: Human-readable display name for the component
requiredFields.purpose: One-line description of what the component does and its primary use case
requiredFields.layer: L1-L6 classification with detailed justification for placement
requiredFields.category: One of: general, containers, inputs, navigation, data-display, composed
requiredFields.dependencies: List of existing components this depends on or relates to
requiredFields.justification: Detailed explanation of why this cannot be achieved by composing existing components
requiredFields.requestedBy: Name and role of who requested this (product, engineering, design, AI agent)
requiredFields.targetProducts: Which products or features need this component
requiredFields.estimatedComplexity: Low, Medium, or High based on implementation effort
requiredFields.estimatedDeliveryDate: Target completion date for full release

validationRules: Name must not conflict with any existing component in the registry
Must demonstrate with specific examples that existing components cannot satisfy the need through composition
Layer assignment must respect dependency rules: cannot depend on same or higher layer components
Must identify at least one target product or specific feature that needs this component
Justification must reference attempted composition patterns and their limitations
Category must be appropriate for the component's function and role in the system
Proposer must have documented current use case in at least one product
outputs: proposal-document.md with all required fields completed

approval.criteria: Proposal meets all validation rules and demonstrates genuine unmet need

phases.name: Specification
phases.description: Detailed component specification is written including API, behavior, states, and accessibility requirements

requiredFields.api: Complete prop interface with TypeScript types
requiredFields.variants: All supported variants and their combinations
requiredFields.states: Default, hover, focus, active, disabled, loading, error, success states
requiredFields.accessibility: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance requirements and ARIA attributes
requiredFields.interactions: User interactions and expected behaviors for each state
requiredFields.composition: Whether component is atomic or composed of subcomponents
requiredFields.subcomponents: List of internal subcomponents if applicable
requiredFields.tokens: Design tokens used for spacing, color, typography, sizing
requiredFields.responsive: Behavior at each breakpoint and container query rules
requiredFields.contentGuidelines: When to use, when not to use, dos and donts
requiredFields.relatedComponents: Similar or related components and differentiation
requiredFields.migrationPath: If replacing existing component, migration strategy from old to new

validationRules: API must be minimal and expressive, avoiding verbose or redundant props
All states must be testable and have clear visual differentiation
Accessibility requirements must include keyboard navigation and screen reader support
Variants must follow established naming conventions from existing components
Design tokens must be used instead of hardcoded values
Responsive behavior must be defined for all breakpoints
Content guidelines must include real-world examples
Subcomponents must be documented with their own APIs if composed
Must pass U1-U19 audit template requirements for structure

review.criteria: Specification is complete, consistent with design system, technically feasible

phases.name: Human Approval
phases.description: Specification is reviewed and explicitly approved by human stakeholder before implementation begins

requiredFields.specificationId: Reference to completed specification from phase 2
requiredFields.approvalComments: Any feedback or conditions for approval
requiredFields.designGoalAlignment: How this component aligns with design system goals
requiredFields.businessValue: Expected business impact and user benefit
requiredFields.technicalFeasibility: Confirmation that implementation is feasible within constraints
requiredFields.resourceAllocation: Approved resources and timeline for implementation

validationRules: Approver must have authority to approve new components for their product
Approval must be explicit and documented (not implicit or assumed)
Specification must not have unresolved comments or questions
All previous phase validation must pass
Approver must confirm alignment with product roadmap and design goals
Cannot proceed without explicit sign-off - no defaults or assumptions
outputs: approval-record.json with approver details and date

approval.criteria: Human has reviewed specification and explicitly approves proceeding to implementation
approval.evidenceRequired: Recorded approval with timestamp and approver identity

phases.name: Implementation
phases.description: Component is implemented in code following specification exactly, with tests and internal documentation

requiredFields.componentFile: Main component file path and location
requiredFields.subcomponentFiles: Paths to all subcomponent files if composed
requiredFields.testFile: Test file with minimum 80% code coverage
requiredFields.storybookStories: Storybook stories for all variants and states
requiredFields.designTokenUsage: All styling via design tokens, no hardcoded values
requiredFields.accessibilityImplementation: ARIA attributes, keyboard navigation, role definitions
requiredFields.responsiveImplementation: CSS media queries and container queries per spec

validationRules: Implementation must match specification exactly - no deviations without approval
All props and variants from spec must be implemented
All states must be testable via Storybook
No hardcoded values - all sizing, spacing, color must use design tokens
Test coverage must be at least 80% of component code
TypeScript types must match specification interface exactly
Keyboard navigation must work per accessibility spec
Must pass automated U1-U19 audit
Code must follow established style guide and patterns from existing components
Responsive behavior must work at all specified breakpoints
outputs: component.stories.tsx with all variants and states

review.criteria: U1-U19 audit passes all checks, no hardcoded values, tests pass, coverage above 80%
review.automationLevel: Fully automated with human review gate

phases.name: Documentation
phases.description: All documentation is written and integrated into design system knowledge base and registry

requiredFields.designSystemRegistry: Component entry in registry with all metadata
requiredFields.componentDocumentation: Full documentation page with usage examples
requiredFields.apiReference: Complete API reference with prop descriptions and types
requiredFields.contentGuidelines: Dos and donts with real-world examples
requiredFields.accessibilityGuide: WCAG compliance notes and keyboard navigation guide
requiredFields.designTokens: List of all design tokens used by component
requiredFields.codeExamples: Working code examples for common use cases
requiredFields.relatedComponentsGuide: How this component relates to similar components
requiredFields.migrationGuide: If replacing existing component, detailed migration steps
requiredFields.roadmapEntry: Updated roadmap with component release date and status

validationRules: All documentation must be written in clear, concise language
Code examples must be functional and tested
API reference must match implementation exactly
Content guidelines must include specific visual examples
Accessibility documentation must reference WCAG success criteria
Design token usage must match implementation
Related components section must be bidirectional - all related components updated
Registry entry must include version, layer, category, and dependencies
All documentation must be internally linked and cross-referenced
Roadmap must be updated before component can be released to users

review.criteria: All required documentation complete, accurate, well-organized, examples tested

phases.name: Release
phases.description: Component is packaged, versioned, and released to public component library and design system

requiredFields.releaseNotes: Summary of what is included in this release
requiredFields.changelog: Detailed list of changes, fixes, and improvements
requiredFields.npmPackage: Published to npm under design system package
requiredFields.figmaLibrary: Design component added to Figma design library
requiredFields.deprecationNotices: Notices for any replaced components or patterns
requiredFields.releaseDate: Date component was released to users

validationRules: All documentation must be live and accessible before release
npm package must include TypeScript types
Changelog must be complete and accurate
Breaking changes must be clearly documented
Version number must follow semantic versioning
Figma design library must be updated with design component
All tests must pass in CI/CD pipeline
No known critical bugs at time of release
Release notes must explain what changed and why
Migration guides must be in place for replaced components
outputs: git tag vX.Y.Z created and pushed

review.criteria: All documentation live, package published, no critical bugs, ready for general use

phases.name: Deprecation
phases.description: Component is phased out when replaced by newer version, with clear migration path for users

requiredFields.sunsetDate: Date after which component will no longer be supported
requiredFields.replacementComponent: Name of component that replaces this one
requiredFields.migrationGuide: Step-by-step guide for migrating to replacement
requiredFields.notificationStrategy: How users are notified of deprecation
requiredFields.supportWindow: How long old version will receive bug fixes
requiredFields.reasons: Explanation of why component is being deprecated

validationRules: Replacement component must be fully documented and released
Migration guide must be specific and include code examples
Support window must be minimum 6 months from deprecation date
Users must be notified at least 3 months before sunset date
Automated migration scripts should be provided if possible
All references to component in docs must be updated
Deprecation notice must appear in component documentation
Changelog must record deprecation with sunset date
outputs: announcement sent to all product teams
knowledge-graph updated to mark as deprecated

review.criteria: Replacement component ready, migration guide complete, users notified

roles.proposer: Any team member or AI agent who identifies a component need
roles.spec-writer: Design system copilot agent or design system team member
roles.approver: Human stakeholder with decision authority (design lead or product owner)
roles.implementer: Frontend engineer or design system copilot agent
roles.documentation-team: Technical writer or design system team member
roles.release-manager: Design system team lead or product manager
roles.automated-audit: U1-U19 audit system that validates implementation
roles.design-lead: Design system lead with approval authority
roles.product-owner: Product owner for the product using the component

statusTransitions.requires: Proposal accepted and approved by design lead or product owner
statusTransitions.condition: All validation rules for proposal phase pass

statusTransitions.requires: Specification passes peer review and is complete
statusTransitions.condition: Specification document is comprehensive and all sections completed

statusTransitions.requires: Explicit human approval on record from authorized approver
statusTransitions.condition: Approval timestamp and approver identity documented in governance registry

statusTransitions.requires: Implementation passes U1-U19 audit with no critical issues
statusTransitions.condition: Automated audit passes, tests pass, no hardcoded values, coverage above 80%

statusTransitions.requires: All documentation complete and live on design system site
statusTransitions.condition: Registry updated, roadmap updated, all docs linked and cross-referenced

statusTransitions.requires: No critical bugs reported for minimum 2 weeks after release
statusTransitions.condition: Component in production use by at least one product team

statusTransitions.requires: Replacement component released and migration path documented
statusTransitions.condition: Replacement component fully documented and available to users

emergencyProcess.description: Fast-track approval process for critical bugs or security fixes in existing released components
emergencyProcess.applicableTo: Critical bugs, security vulnerabilities, data loss issues, compliance violations
steps: Identify and verify issue is critical
Get verbal or written approval from release manager or design lead
Run U1-U19 audit to verify no new issues introduced
Document incident and post-mortem within 48 hours

rules.noComponentWithoutApproval: No component may be created or added to the design system without explicit human approval in the human-approval phase
rules.allChangesTracked: All component changes must be tracked in version control with clear commit messages
rules.noBreakingChanges: No breaking API changes in stable components without major version bump and deprecation period
rules.designTokensMandatory: All styling must use design tokens - no hardcoded values permitted
rules.testingRequired: All components must have 80%+ code coverage with passing tests
rules.accessibilityRequired: All components must meet WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility standards
rules.documentationRequired: All components must have complete documentation before release
rules.typesRequired: All components must have complete TypeScript type definitions

metrics.averageProposalToReleaseTime: Target: 60 days from proposal to general availability
metrics.componentCoverage: Target: 90%+ of UI patterns covered by documented components
metrics.adoptionRate: Track percentage of new components used by product teams within first 3 months
metrics.qualityScore: All released components must have U1-U19 audit score of 90 or higher
metrics.accessibilityCompliance: All components must pass WCAG 2.1 AA audit with 0 critical issues
